title: Acacia morrisonii
intro: Acacia morrisonii, also known as Philippine acacia, is a species of tree native to the Philippines, characterized by its thorny branches and fragrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Acacia morrisonii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Acacia morrisonii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Acacia, which consists of over 1,300 species. The species is native to the Philippines and is a type of tree or shrub. It is a member of the subgenus Phyllodineae, which is a group of Acacia species characterized by bipinnate or pinnate leaves. Acacia morrisonii is closely related to other tropical tree species in the Fabaceae family.

These compounds are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties and have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The flavonoids present in Acacia morrisonii have been identified as kaempferol, quercetin, and isorhapontigenin, which have been reported to have potential health benefits.
